text:
Long ago, deep in the heart of an ancient forest, there lived an old and wise creature known only as Old Man Willow. Willow's branches were as thick as trees, and his bark was as weathered as stone. He guarded the enchanted forest with a fierce loyalty.

One autumn evening, as shadows extended long across the forest floor, a humble traveler stumbled upon Willow’s brooding presence. The traveler spoke boldly, though his voice shook ever so slightly—a child lost from his woodlands home.

"Old Man Willow, why do such great shadows gather over your forest?"

Willow didnouldn't speak. Instead, he watched curiously as the traveler began to wonder aloud about forest sprites and lost little ones. Finally, with a small rustle of his bark, Willow uttered something unmistakably old-fashioned.

"My cleverest sprite has taken off with a youngling from the human village. These foolish humans always try to steal our forest children."
